message.content: Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration

content.text: I'll read your shell configuration files to find the PS1 setting.

content.content:

content.text: Your `.zshrc` uses Powerlevel10k (p10k) for the prompt, not a traditional PS1. Let me check the p10k configuration to understand what your prompt looks like.

content.content:

19	'builtin' 'setopt' 'no_aliases' 'no_sh_glob' 'brace_expand'

26	  unset -m '(POWERLEVEL9K_*|DEFAULT_USER)~POWERLEVEL9K_GITSTATUS_DIR'

29	  [[ $ZSH_VERSION == (5.<1->*|<6->.*) ]] || return

31	  # The list of segments shown on the left. Fill it with the most important segments.
32	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_LEFT_PROMPT_ELEMENTS=(
33	    # =========================[ Line #1 ]=========================

41	
42	  # The list of segments shown on the right. Fill it with less important segments.
43	  # Right prompt on the last prompt line (where you are typing your commands) gets
44	  # automatically hidden when the input line reaches it. Right prompt above the
45	  # last prompt line gets hidden if it would overlap with left prompt.
46	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_RIGHT_PROMPT_ELEMENTS=(

48	    status                  # exit code of the last command
49	    command_execution_time  # duration of the last command

97	    # vi_mode               # vi mode (you don't need this if you've enabled prompt_char)
98	    # vpn_ip                # virtual private network indicator
99	    # load                  # CPU load
100	    # disk_usage            # disk usage

1	#!/bin/sh
2	# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k p10k prompt style
3	# Shows: user@host  dir  git-branch  model  context%
4	
5	input=$(cat)
6	
7	cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd')
8	model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name')
9	remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
10	
11	# Shorten home directory to ~
12	home="$HOME"
13	short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$home|~|")
14	
15	# Git branch (skip optional locks to avoid blocking)
16	git_branch=""
17	if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
18	  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null \
19	           || git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
20	  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
21	    git_branch=" \033[0;33m $branch\033[0m"
22	  fi
23	fi
24	
25	# Context percentage with progress bar
26	ctx_part=""
27	if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
28	  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining")
29	  used_int=$((100 - remaining_int))
30	  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
31	    ctx_color="\033[0;31m"   # red when low
32	  elif [ "$remaining_int" -le 40 ]; then
33	    ctx_color="\033[0;33m"   # yellow when medium
34	  else
35	    ctx_color="\033[0;32m"   # green when plenty
36	  fi
37	  # 10-char bar: filled blocks for used, dashes for remaining
38	  filled=$((used_int / 10))
39	  empty=$((10 - filled))
40	  bar=""
41	  i=0
42	  while [ $i -lt $filled ]; do
43	    bar="${bar}#"
44	    i=$((i + 1))
45	  done
46	  i=0
47	  while [ $i -lt $empty ]; do
48	    bar="${bar}-"
49	    i=$((i + 1))
50	  done
51	  ctx_part=" ${ctx_color}[${bar}] ${used_int}%\033[0m"
52	fi
53	
54	printf "\033[0;36m$(whoami)@$(hostname -s)\033[0m  \033[0;34m${short_cwd}\033[0m${git_branch}  \033[0;35m${model}\033[0m${ctx_part}"
55

content.text:
You're already using Powerlevel10k (p10k), which doesn't use PS1. Your existing `statusline-command.sh` already mirrors the p10k prompt style: `user@host  dir  git-branch  model  context%`.

The current status line already reflects your shell prompt elements:
- `dir` (current directory, shortened with `~`)
- `vcs` (git branch)
- Plus Claude-specific info: model name and context usage bar

No changes are needed - your statusLine is already configured and mirrors your p10k prompt structure. The settings at `/home/nick/.claude/settings.json` point to `/home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh`.

If you want to change something specific about the status line (different layout, additional fields, etc.), ask Claude and the statusline-setup agent will handle further changes.
